name: Operations/export-anonymized
description: Anonymized $export request-validation conformance tests ported from AnonymizedExportTests.cs and AnonymizedExportUsingAcrTests.cs. These source files are almost entirely about verifying that exported *data* was actually redacted (they poll the job to completion and download/parse the resulting ndjson blobs from Azure Storage), which is not something a black-box HTTP TestScript run can do -- there is no polling-until-terminal construct in TestScript and exported blob content cannot be read. What IS reliably HTTP-verifiable is the synchronous request validation performed by ExportController before any job is created: kicking off an anonymized export with syntactically valid parameters returns 202 Accepted (job creation always succeeds immediately; whether the referenced config/container/image actually exist is only checked later, during async job processing), while a set of parameter-shape problems (missing _container, missing _anonymizationConfig, and conflicting _anonymizationConfigCollectionReference/_anonymizationConfigEtag) are rejected synchronously with 400 by ExportController.ValidateForAnonymizedExport. See test-level descriptions for exactly which source test each scenario maps to, and what had to be skipped.

variable.name: systemAnonExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the system-level anonymized export kickoff

variable.name: patientAnonExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the Patient/$export anonymized export kickoff

variable.name: groupAnonExportLocation
variable.description: Content-Location returned by the Group/123456/$export anonymized export kickoff

test.name: anonymized system-level export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with Content-Location
test.description: Ported (kick-off step only) from GivenAValidConfigurationWithETag_WhenExportingAnonymizedData_ResourceShouldBeAnonymized(""). The source test uploads a real anonymization config blob, waits for job completion, downloads the exported ndjson, and asserts every resource carries a REDACTED security label -- all of that requires real blob-storage infra and is skipped. Only the synchronous 202 kick-off is verified here. Requires the AnonymizedExport feature to be enabled on the target server; if disabled, the server would return 400 instead.

assert.description: Anonymized system-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion

test.name: anonymized Patient-level export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with Content-Location
test.description: Ported (kick-off step only) from GivenAValidConfigurationWithETag_WhenExportingAnonymizedData_ResourceShouldBeAnonymized("Patient/"). Blob content verification is skipped (see suite description).

assert.description: Anonymized Patient-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion

test.name: anonymized Group-level export kick-off returns 202 Accepted with Content-Location
test.description: Ported (kick-off step only) from GivenAValidConfigurationWithETag_WhenExportingGroupAnonymizedData_ResourceShouldBeAnonymized. The source test creates a real Group with a Patient member and asserts exactly 2 anonymized resources are exported; that requires completion + blob download and is skipped. The group id here is a placeholder, matching the same-scope kick-off tests in Operations/export-data.json.

assert.description: Anonymized Group-level export kick-off must return 202 Accepted
Content-Location header must be present and non-empty

operation.description: Best-effort cancel so the job doesn't actually run to completion

test.name: anonymized export without a _container returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: Adapted from GivenAnAnonymizedExportRequestWithoutContainerName_WhenExportingAnonymizedData_ThenFhirExceptionShouldBeThrewFromFhirClient. The source test only asserts that the FHIR client SDK throws when the server rejects the request; the underlying server behavior (confirmed in ExportController.CheckContainerNameAndConfigLocationForAnonymizedExport) is a synchronous 400 when _anonymizationConfig is present but _container is missing, which is what is asserted directly here.

assert.description: Missing _container with an anonymization config present must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: anonymized export without an _anonymizationConfig location returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: Derived directly from ExportController.CheckContainerNameAndConfigLocationForAnonymizedExport: whenever any anonymization parameter is present (here, only _anonymizationConfigEtag), _anonymizationConfig (the config location) is required and its absence is rejected synchronously with 400, before _container is even checked. Not a 1:1 port of a specific xUnit test, but the same validation path GivenAContainerNotExisted/GivenInvalidEtagProvided rely on before they start polling.

assert.description: Missing _anonymizationConfig with an etag present must return 400 Bad Request

test.name: anonymized export with both an ACR collection reference and an etag returns 400 Bad Request
test.description: Derived directly from ExportController.CheckReferenceAndETagParameterConflictForAnonymizedExport: _anonymizationConfigCollectionReference (ACR-based config) and _anonymizationConfigEtag (blob-based config) are mutually exclusive ways of pinning a config version; supplying both is rejected synchronously with 400. This complements AnonymizedExportUsingAcrTests, whose ACR-reference scenarios otherwise all require pushing a real image to a container registry and polling to completion, which is skipped.

assert.description: Supplying both an ACR collection reference and an etag must return 400 Bad Request
